passes.notes: Read the copied SQNT-03D route connector and condition-rider facts. The dirty replay is limited to the modeled shape families named there: attack-hit Poisoned, failed-save Blinded, repeat-save Blinded or Deafened cleanup, Restrained Athletics escape, Sleep Incapacitated to Unconscious transition, and condition-immunity rejection.

Runtime vocabulary uses condition, boundary, immunity, repeat-save frontier, escape check, transition, active effect, condition lifecycle, and turn boundary language from copied cleanroom inputs. Authored selected spell names remain in adapter/evidence only.

Condition kind, duration boundary, lifecycle phase, and route subject sequencing are localized in BattleConditionRiderCondition, BattleConditionRiderBoundary, BattleConditionRiderEffect, BattleSubjectKind, and reducer transition helpers.

The focused adapter replays all 16 copied route connector actions. The route connector is the lane authority and is recorded from reducer-route inventory; the selected condition-saving driver is used only for downstream source-branch evidence checking of mapped obligations. Level-1 damage spell selected identity remains residual context because selecting the whole driver would pull unrelated damage-spell obligations into this lane.
The reducer owns durable lifecycle and immunity state. The adapter only selects copied connector action names and constructs expected route facts from copied connector vocabulary.
Copied QNT action names and selected-identity spell branch names appear in the focused adapter, tests, and evidence artifacts only.
Production changes are scoped to battle reducer route subjects, route-family projection, typed condition-rider state, and combatant condition-immunity facts.

BattleSpellActiveEffects.condition_rider is not derivable after host resolution because later turn-boundary, repeat-save, transition, and cleanup subjects need durable lifecycle phase. Combatant.condition_immunities is a target fact, not authored identity.

Production reducers dispatch on typed route subjects, condition facts, boundary facts, fill kinds, and BattleState phase. No production branch dispatches on spell names, ids, slugs, source headings, page refs, or official catalog labels.
Evidence is labeled diagnostic dirty replay. Hold Person and Hideous Laughter lifecycle shapes are recorded as source-QNT blockers, not inferred from selected identity.
Added condition_rider_active_effect_cleanup_requires_lifecycle_cleanup to reject active-effect cleanup before lifecycle cleanup and preserve the active condition-rider state.
The strong coupling between condition-rider phase and legal follow-up subjects is executable in reducer stale-subject checks and transition helpers, with one focused regression for premature cleanup.
Second pass found no reasonable additional findings. The only intentionally selected branch blocker is Hideous Laughter, which the copied SQNT-03D source names as out of modeled route scope.
